logic_ruling.disproof_requires: exhibit g making output CLOSE to LPN; needs I(e';C)=0 as a NECESSARY ingredient
logic_ruling.kimi_found: cannot reach I=0 under marginal-uniform C (min ~0.99 n=2, ~1.72 n=3, growing)
logic_ruling.correct_inference: failing to reach the necessary ingredient SUPPORTS lem:m2 (output stays far from LPN); it does NOT disprove it
logic_ruling.kimi_inference: 'if no g gives I=0 then lem:m2 false' -- SIGN INVERTED
logic_ruling.n_growth: leakage growing with n = output increasingly far from LPN = PRO-security (Kimi read it as anti)

quantity_ruling.operative: distinguishing advantage of an algorithm seeing only (C,z), with B=g(A) and x UNKNOWN, one fresh A per instance
quantity_ruling.I(e_prime;C): hands e' (hence ~B) to the adversary; over-strong, not the distinguisher's view
quantity_ruling.support_note: z=By in colspace(B) (dim<=2n); but ONE z reveals no low-dim structure without B (paper's 'solver doesn't know B' obstruction) -> I>0 != distinguishing attack
